name: Black Magic Cake (Crs)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, sift together flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
2. Add eggs, vegetable oil, strong brew coffee (cooled), buttermilk, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients.
3. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed for 3 minutes until well combined.
4. Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean.
7. Allow the cake to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
8. Frost the cooled cake with Hundred Dollar Frosting (see below).
9. Cut into squares.
Hundred Dollar Frosting:
1. Melt butter and semisweet chocolate together in a double boiler over hot water, stirring until smooth.
2. Remove from heat and allow to cool for a few minutes.
3. Add egg and stir vigorously until well combined.
4. Stir in confectioners' sugar, vanilla extract, and lemon juice until the mixture is smooth.
5. Fold in chopped walnuts.
6. Frost the cooled cake with the icing.
Source: CRS file
